'''James Clare''' (born 13 April 1991) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|fb}} in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship]] in the 2010s and 2020s.

Clare spent the majority of his career with the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the Super League across two spells, making over 100 appearances and scoring over 50 tries. He played two seasons for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the Championship, and also represented the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the Super League. He spent time on loan or dual registration with [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and the [[York City Knights]] in the Championship, and at the [[York City Knights]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

==Background==
Clare was born in [[Castleford]], West Yorkshire, England.

Having come through the Castleford Tigers academy system, Clare earned his first full-time contract prior to the 2012 season.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Tigers academy trio earn full time contracts |url=http://www.superleaguefans.com/castleford-tigers/2011/10/07/tigers-academy-trio-earn-full-time-contracts.html |access-date=2020-04-24 |website=Superleaguefans.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=23 September 2020 |title=Video RL Stories with James Clare: "How am I in a room with Willie Tonga?!" |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/video-rl-stories-with-james-clare-how-am-i-in-a-room-with-willie-tonga |access-date=27 December 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Castleford Tigers===
Clare made his first team début for the [[Castleford Tigers]] on 16 March 2012 away to the [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/17354421|title=Huddersfield 42-4 Castleford|date=2012-03-16|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-24|language=en-gb}}</ref> He made 2 further appearances that season, and scored his first try on 8 July 2012 at home to Huddersfield.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18683329|title=Castleford Tigers 52-6 Huddersfield Giants|date=2012-07-07|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-24|language=en-gb}}</ref>

In June 2013, Clare signed a two-year contract extension with the Tigers.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/james-clare-re-signs-with-castleford/|title=James Clare re-signs with Castleford|date=2013-06-28|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref> On 8 September 2013, Clare scored 4 tries in Castleford's visit to local rivals [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] on the final day of the season.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23953167|title=Wakefield 36-32 Castleford|date=2013-09-08|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-24|language=en-gb}}</ref> In 2013, he made 9 appearances and scored 8 tries.

Clare was allocated squad number 24 prior to the [[2014 Castleford Tigers season|2014 season]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-2014-squad-numbers/|title=Castleford Tigers 2014 squad numbers|date=2013-12-19|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref> He made 17 appearances and scored 11 tries for Castleford as the team finished 4th in [[Super League]] and reached the [[2014 Challenge Cup final|Challenge Cup final]].

Clare was given squad number 2 for 2015, following the retirement of [[Kirk Dixon]], after his impressive performances in 2014 established him in the first team.<ref name="First team">{{Cite web|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=3820|title=Clare's ready to face the Wildcats|date=2015-02-03|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref> Despite this, Clare said, "The hard work starts now in my eyes. The number will mean nothing if you don't train as hard as you can or perform on the day".<ref name="First team" /> He made 9 appearances and scored 4 tries for the Tigers, as well as playing for the [[York City Knights]] on dual registration, before his move to the [[Bradford Bulls]] was announced on 22 July.<ref name="Sticks" />

==== Doncaster (loan) ====
Clare joined newly-promoted [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] for the 2013 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=1578|title=Tigers Duo Out on Loan|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref> He was to be on a 24 hour call back for parent club Castleford after 28 days. Clare made 4 appearances and scored 1 try for the club across February and March before returning to the Tigers.<ref name="Love Rugby League">{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/James-Clare/|title=James Clare Player Stats|website=Love Rugby League|access-date=2020-04-24|archive-date=10 December 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181210154707/https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/james-clare/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==== York City Knights (dual registration) ====
In 2015, Clare played for the [[York City Knights]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]] through their dual registration arrangement with Castleford. The Tigers' head coach [[Daryl Powell]] said he was "looking for him to build some confidence" through spending time at York.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/9829781/castleford-winger-james-clare-off-to-league-one-york|title=Castleford winger James Clare off to League One York|website=Sky Sports|language=en|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref> Clare made 7 appearances and scored 9 tries for the Knights.<ref name="Love Rugby League" />

===Bradford Bulls===
Clare signed for Championship side the [[Bradford Bulls]] in July, towards the end of the 2015 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-sign-james-clare-and-matt-ryan/|title=Bradford sign James Clare and Matt Ryan|date=2015-07-23|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref> He signed a deal until the end of the 2016 season.

In the [[2015 Bradford Bulls season|2015 Season]], Clare featured in Round 23 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]). Clare played in Qualifier 1 ([[Sheffield Eagles]]) to Qualifier 4 ([[Widnes Vikings]]) then in Qualifier 6 ([[Leigh Centurions]]) to Qualifier 7 (Halifax). He scored against the Sheffield Eagles (1 try), the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] (1 try) and [[Salford Red Devils]] (1 try).

In the [[2016 Bradford Bulls season|2016 Season]], Clare featured in the pre-season friendlies against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] and the [[Castleford Tigers]]. He scored against Castleford Tigers (1 try). He featured in Round 1 ([[Featherstone Rovers]]) to Round 22 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]). Clare played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in the 4th Round ([[Dewsbury Rams]]). He scored against [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] (2 tries), the [[Swinton Lions]] (3 tries), the Leigh Centurions (3 tries), the [[London Broncos]] (1 try), Halifax (1 try), the Sheffield Eagles (1 try), the Dewsbury Rams (1 try), [[Workington Town]] (2 tries), Oldham (2 tries) and the [[Batley Bulldogs]] (2 tries). In June, midway through the season, Clare signed a contract extension keeping him at Bradford until 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/bradford-duo-james-clare-omari-caro-sign-new-deals/|title=Bradford duo James Clare and Omari Caro sign new deals|last=Bower|first=Aaron|date=2016-06-06|website=Total Rugby League|language=en-US|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref>

Clare later recalled that, after Bradford were placed in administration in November 2016, he "seriously considered quitting the game for good", saying, "The amount of emotional stress you go through takes its toll on you physically in the end."<ref>{{Cite news|last=Heppenstall|first=Ross|url=https://www.express.co.uk/sport/othersport/938495/Castleford-Tigers-James-Clare|title=EXCLUSIVE: Castleford winger James Clare reveals all on his tumultuous journey|date=2018-03-29|work=The Daily Express|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref> In January 2017, following the Bradford Bulls' liquidation, Clare became a free agent.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/38418199|title=Bradford Bulls: Former Super League champions liquidated|date=2017-01-03|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-24|language=en-gb}}</ref>

=== Leigh Centurions ===
On 5 January 2017, it was announced that Clare had signed a 2-year deal with the newly-promoted [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/v2/blog/2017/01/05/centurions-confirm-james-clare-signing/|title=Centurions Confirm James Clare Signing|date=2017-01-05|website=Leigh Centurions|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref> Leigh head coach [[Neil Jukes]] said: "James is someone we've admired and monitored for some while now, especially after his performance in our epic draw at Bradford last year."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/34439/10719515/james-clare-joins-leigh-centurions-from-bradford-bulls|title=James Clare joins Leigh Centurions from Bradford Bulls|date=2017-01-06|website=Sky Sports|language=en|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref> He was allocated shirt number 28, due to arriving after the squad numbers were revealed.

Unfortunately, Clare suffered an [[anterior cruciate ligament injury]] in Leigh's pre-season win over the [[Dewsbury Rams]] on 29 January 2017.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/long-term-injury-blow-rules-out-former-castleford-tigers-star-james-clare-604809|title=Long-term injury blow rules out former Castleford Tigers star James Clare|date=2017-02-08|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk|language=en|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref> This ruled him out for most of the upcoming season. He made his first appearance for the Centurions on 3 September against the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the fourth round of the [[The Qualifiers|Qualifiers]], scoring a try.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41089670|title=Qualifiers: Leigh Centurions 8-24 Widnes Vikings|date=2017-09-03|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-24|language=en-gb}}</ref> He played the remainder of the season, making 5 appearances and scoring 6 tries.<ref name="Love Rugby League" /> Leigh were defeated by the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the 2017 [[Million Pound Game]], resulting in their relegation to the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and meaning all contracts became null and void, leaving other clubs free to approach Clare.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41402866|title=Million Pound Game: Leigh Centurions 10-26 Catalans Dragons|date=2017-09-30|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-24|language=en-gb}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
In December 2017, Clare rejoined his hometown club [[Castleford Tigers]] on a one-year deal for the 2018 season, with an option to extend the deal by a further year.<ref name="Cas return">{{cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5745|title=Clare returns to Cas|date=5 December 2017|website=Castleford Tigers|accessdate=8 December 2017}}</ref> He said, "Cas are the club that I've supported for my entire life so to be back here and to have another chance to pull on this famous shirt is great," and added, "I pushed for the move as much as I could because I knew I always wanted to come back here."<ref name="Cas return" />

In his first season back at Castleford, Clare wore the number 26.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5773|title=2018 Squad Numbers|date=2018-01-23|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-04-24}}</ref> After beginning the season at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] on dual registration, he played his first game since returning against the [[Salford Red Devils]] on 11 March, scoring a try.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/43306161|title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 22-8 Salford Red Devils|date=2018-03-11|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-24|language=en-gb}}</ref> He was kept out for a number of months mid-season due to a [[tibial plateau fracture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5915|title=Injury update|date=2018-05-03|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref> In July, he extended his contract until the end of the 2020 season.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44779722|title=James Clare: Castleford Tigers winger signs contract extension|date=2018-07-10|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-25|language=en-gb}}</ref> Despite his injury – and the fierce competition for places on Castleford's wings from [[Greg Eden]], [[Greg Minikin]] and [[Jy Hitchcox]] – he made 18 appearances and scored 8 tries.<ref name="Love Rugby League" />

[[File:James Clare Castleford.jpg|thumb|right|Clare warming up for the Castleford Tigers at [[Anfield]] in 2019]]
The [[2019 Castleford Tigers season|2019 campaign]] proved to be Clare's most prolific season yet in the [[Super League]]. He regained squad number 2, claiming the right-wing berth as his own following Greg Minikin's move to centre.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6202|title=2019 squad numbers confirmed|date=2019-01-04|website=Castleford Tigers|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref> He made 30 appearances, playing in all but 2 of Castleford's matches, and scored 16 tries, topping the club's try-scoring charts.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/james-clare/summary.html|title=James Clare - Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref> This included his second hat-trick for the Tigers on 12 July – this was his first Castleford treble since 2013 and it occurred, again, in a visit to local rivals [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/48920133|title=Super League: Castleford beat Wakefield 36-16 in derby thanks to James Clare hat-trick|date=2019-07-12|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-25|language=en-gb}}</ref> In recognition of his form, Clare also finished the season as runner-up for the club's Players' Player of the Year award.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6719|title=Boxing Day talking points|date=2019-12-20|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref>

Following the arrival of [[Sosaia Feki]] and [[Derrell Olpherts]] at Castleford for 2020, Clare was given squad number 21.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6748|title=2020 Squad Numbers Revealed|date=2020-01-17|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref> He made a strong start to the campaign and had made 5 appearances and scored 4 tries when the [[Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on rugby league|season was suspended in March]]. Upon the season's resumption, injuries to teammates saw Clare fill in at fullback and centre.<ref name="Cas extension">{{Cite web |date=2020-12-09 |title=Clare sticks with Cas in 2021 |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7194 |access-date=2022-10-11 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> In December 2020, it was announced that Clare had signed a two-year extension with Castleford,<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-01-30 |title=Clare on Stick-ing around with Cas |url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7244 |access-date=2022-10-11 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> with head coach [[Daryl Powell]] touting him as the team's "most improved player" and praising his hard work and commitment.<ref name="Cas extension" /><ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-12-09 |title=James Clare: Castleford winger signs new one-year contract |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55250529 |access-date=2022-10-11 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Prior to the 2021 season, Clare was forced to undergo knee surgery due to an injury sustained during the previous campaign.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=2021-02-10 |title=Castleford Tigers winger James Clare remaining positive despite injury setback |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-james-clare-injury-19808380 |access-date=2022-10-11 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref> This caused him to miss the start of the year, and he was limited to 7 appearances throughout the season. First-team opportunities proved harder to come by for Clare in 2022 under [[Lee Radford]], making 3 appearances, though he was a part of the coaching staff for the club's academy.<ref name="Cas exit">{{Cite web |date=2022-08-26 |title=Tigers departing club at end of 2022 |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8260 |access-date=2022-10-11 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> On 26 August 2022, Castleford confirmed that Clare would leave the club at the end of the season upon the expiry of his contract.<ref name="Cas exit" /><ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=2022-08-26 |title=Castleford confirm six departures at the end of the season |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-six-players-leaving/ |access-date=2022-10-11 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> Across both stints with Castleford, Clare made 108 appearances and scored 54 tries.

==== Halifax (dual registration) ====
In February 2018, Clare played for [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] through their dual registration agreement with Castleford.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-keeping-tabs-progress-loan-men-332667|title=Castleford keeping tabs on progress of loan men|date=2018-03-18|work=Pontefract and Castleford Express|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref> He made 3 appearances.<ref name="Love Rugby League" />

==== York City Knights (loan) ====
In February 2022, Clare joined [[RFL Championship|Championship]] team [[York City Knights|York]] on a one-month loan deal.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gordon |first=James |date=2022-02-18 |title=Castleford winger James Clare makes return to York |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-winger-james-clare-makes-return-to-york/ |access-date=2022-05-23 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> However, he only made one appearance for the Knights after receiving a three-match ban for punching in a pre-season friendly for Castleford.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-01-26 |title=James Clare: Castleford Tigers winger to miss start of season after punching ban |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60138529 |access-date=2022-05-23 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

== Retirement ==
Clare retired from professional rugby league following the conclusion of the [[2022 Castleford Tigers season|2022 season]]. He had been offered a new contract by [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] alongside a foundation coaching role but instead opted to focus on his own coaching business, the Elite Rugby Academy, for children aged 4 to 14.<ref name="LRL Retirement">{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=13 December 2022 |title=James Clare on rejecting new Castleford deal, work outside of rugby league & future playing career |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/james-clare-on-rejecting-new-deal-work-outside-of-rugby-league-future-playing-career |access-date=17 January 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He began work in secondary education in late 2022 as a PLC co-ordinator in a [[Pontefract]] school. He has proved popular with all the students and continues to promote rugby league in school. He has moved on and continued to work in education across Castleford.<ref name="LRL Retirement" />
